authority: repository governance projection only; not runtime effectiveness, consumer installation, Hook enablement, project-standard admission, cross-repository mutation, release, commit, or push authority

layerOrder.role: shape context, intent, constraints, and default behavior
layerOrder.boundary: necessary but not sufficient across hosts with different discovery, precedence, context, and instruction-following behavior

layerOrder.role: make reusable capability, orchestration, and composition discoverable
layerOrder.boundary: do not raise a model's native capability ceiling and are not mandatory when native or no-Skill paths are sufficient

layerOrder.role: apply deterministic checks to bounded machine-readable claims
layerOrder.boundary: prove only their declared contract and do not create external authority or live-runtime proof

layerOrder.role: supply optional host-specific recall, enforcement, or automation when evidence justifies stronger control
layerOrder.boundary: remain consumer-owned, mode-bounded, reversible, and separately authorized
override conflicting generic Skill guidance only after admission by the applicable project authority; this repository cannot admit them

layerOrder.role: bound claims, resolve accountable choices, and trigger correction or revalidation
layerOrder.boundary: remain required where risk, permission, project authority, or real-world judgment cannot be delegated

invariants: use the smallest sufficient layer set and add stronger mechanisms only after evidence shows a material gap
native reasoning, no Skill, no Hook, and no additional structure remain valid outcomes
generic Skill guidance defers to an admitted project-owned hard standard when they conflict
this repository may prepare evidence and candidate contracts but cannot admit a project hard standard
deterministic checks do not prove cross-host runtime effectiveness beyond their declared contract
hard-standard extraction remains deferred until stable cross-source, cross-host, cross-task, and feedback evidence plus separate authority exist

keptOpen.reason: consumer-owned dated mapping and behavior evidence remain incomplete
standard candidate extraction is deliberately deferred until the chain matures
operational graph query, migration fixtures, verification, and project admission evidence do not yet exist
cross-mode and live host behavior remains consumer-side evidence

nonActions: no Skill, Recipe, Hook, CI, runtime, or hard-standard implementation
no consumer or project configuration mutation
no standard-candidate extraction or project-standard admission
no release-manifest or approved payload mutation
no cross-repository write, commit, or push
